note: Backfilled 2026-07-10 by Rafael's apply pass: the 2026-07-05 review predates the machine-readable protocol, so its proposals are recorded here retroactively so the 2026-07-12 run's verification loop engages instead of skipping. rm-4 is backlog cleanup from the 06-21/06-28 reviews (same apply pass).

proposals.action: Append learned preference: '- 2026-07-05: in the Weekend "Week in headlines" digest, always anchor a major event to WHEN it happened, not just its current status (2× 👎 on the 2026-07-04 Khamenei bullet, "Missing context on when it hapenned" — it said he "has died" without the 28 Feb death date the daily brief had already carried).'
proposals.evidence: 2× 👎 on 2026-07-04-weekend Khamenei bullet, one with verbatim reason 'Missing context on when it hapenned' (_posts/2026-07-05-evaluator.md, Reader-feedback Proposal A)

proposals.action: Week in headlines: every bullet states what happened AND WHEN it happened (the underlying event date, not just the latest development); name event dates explicitly — 'X died on {date}' / 'the {date} strike' — even when the news peg is a later funeral/anniversary/reaction; carry forward event dates the daily briefs already carried.

Affiliations become best-effort chain: arXiv <arxiv:affiliation> / Semantic Scholar first; on 429/400/not-indexed fall back to the OpenAlex works API (api.openalex.org/works?filter=doi:… or ?search=title) before any named-senior-author web lookup; only mark '(affiliation not listed)' when all fail. Never guess.
proposals.evidence: Semantic Scholar failed all 4 attempts in the 07-05 window (429/400/not-indexed); _posts/2026-07-05-evaluator.md Patch 2 + Open Q2. Recurs every week for <1-week-old arXiv IDs.

proposals.action: Append learned preference: '- 2026-06-28: for "AI/science changes how we think/work" stories, anchor on the underlying study (journal article / preprint), not a Nature blog/news restatement, and avoid sensational framing — distinguish skill-displacement from real deskilling rather than asserting the alarming reading (2× 👎 on 2026-06-19 overview "AI tools erode skills", "read the actual study… please steer away from sensationalism").'
proposals.evidence: Backlog cleanup: proposed _posts/2026-06-21-evaluator.md, re-proposed verbatim _posts/2026-06-28-evaluator.md Proposal A; unapplied for 3 evaluator cycles until 2026-07-10.
